xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfCoverageAndImplementa: Yes, all our units are maintaining zero liquid discharge. Effluent generated at our smelters is treated in effluent treatment plant (ETP), followed by two stages RO plant. The treated effluents conform to the prescribed standards and recycle in the process. Further, to strengthen zero liquid discharge (ZLD), improved water recovery is achieved using upgraded technology by installation of multiple effect evaporator (MEE)/mechanical vapour recompression (MVR).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyIfAnyIn: The indicators/information disclosed in the BRSR report have been externally assured. BRSR Core indicators obtained Reasonable Assurance and other indicators/information of this report obtained Limited Assurance by S. R. Batliboi & Co. LLP
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: The indicators/information disclosed in the BRSR report have been externally assured. BRSR Core indicators obtained Reasonable Assurance and other indicators/information of this report obtained Limited Assurance by S. R. Batliboi & Co. LLP
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc: Our decarbonisation efforts focus on technology investments, renewable energy adoption, and operational innovation to lower our carbon footprint. Refer to our Sustainability | Energy and Climate Management. Key Highlights of FY2026; • Asia’s first low-carbon ‘green’ zinc, EcoZen, produced using 100% renewable electricity, witnessed expanded adoption during the year as Hindustan Zinc strengthened partnerships with key customers to integrate EcoZen across manufacturing operations, enabling verified Scope 3 emission reductions across one of India’s largest industrial value chains. • Strengthened its sustainable logistics efforts by 52 Electric Vehicle bulkers at its Zinc Smelter Debari in Udaipur. Developed with Enviro Wheels Mobility Pvt. Ltd., ten EV bulkers are already operational, with the rest to be rolled in coming months. • 530 MW round-the-clock renewable energy (RE-RTC) power agreement with Serentica India Renewables Limited through which we received 518 million units power, resulting in reduction of 0.83 million tCO2e
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: The indicators/information disclosed in the BRSR report have been externally assured. BRSR Core indicators obtained Reasonable Assurance and other indicators/information of this report obtained Limited Assurance by S. R. Batliboi & Co. LLP.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: Waste management practices description We have in place a Resource Use and Waste Management Technical Standard and the supporting guidance notes which augment us to mitigate the environmental impacts of our products and process. The Company believes in Zero Waste and has aligned waste management practices to ‘4-R Policy” of Reduce, Reuse, Recycle and Reclaim in our operations. Refer to Sustainability | Waste Management Strategy adopted to reduce usage of hazardous and toxic chemicals in products and processes Company is taking various initiatives and has adopted state-of-the-art technologies to reduce the waste generation, reuse of waste, recovery of metal from waste and disposal of remaining waste in environmentally sound manner. Processes and the practices adopted to manage Hazardous and Toxic Chemicals • The commissioning of Fumer plant has resulted in significant elimination of Jarosite generation from one of the Hydrometallurgical Zinc Smelter and the generated slag will be 100% utilised in cement industries • For effective metal recovery, a second ancillary plant was commissioned for treatment of process residues at Chanderiya Lead-Zinc Smelter • A project to recover sodium sulphate crystal from RO reject was commissioned at Dariba Zinc Smelter • Gainful utilisation of Jarosite, Jarofix, slag and fly ash in cement manufacturing, road construction, and railway embankment as well as tailings in back-filling voids in mines through paste fill/hydrofill For further information, please refer to: Our Approach Note on Responsible Production
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyIfAnyIn: The indicators/information disclosed in the BRSR report have been externally assured. BRSR Core indicators obtained Reasonable Assurance and other indicators/information of this report obtained Limited Assurance by S. R. Batliboi & Co. LLP.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: The indicators/information disclosed in the BRSR report have been externally assured. BRSR Core indicators obtained Reasonable Assurance and other indicators/information of this report obtained Limited Assurance by S. R. Batliboi & Co. LLP.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSignificantDirectAndI: The Company does not have any operations located within notified eco-sensitive zones. While Zawar Mines (ZMW) operations have obtained the requisite forest clearances, all sites of HZL are situated outside ecologically sensitive areas. Comprehensive biodiversity assessments have been conducted across all operational locations, and site specific Biodiversity Management Plans are in place to identify, mitigate, and manage potential direct and indirect impacts on biodiversity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi: All our operating sites are having onsite emergency preparedness plan (including disaster control management plan) along with responsibilities in place covering all the risk associated with the operational activities to mitigate and handle the emergency and ensure business continuity. Emergency scenarios, mock drills, rescue team, and trainings are regularly being carried out for making the system more robust and to check the healthiness of the emergency response procedure. • We have an Incident Management Committee to investigate all the incidents (safety, environment, process) and learnings are being shared across all the locations for horizontal deployment and based on the recommendations, suitable changes are being made in the emergency response plan also. • Stand downs are being conducted on shop floor to communicate the learnings from all such incidents. Regular trainings and awareness programmes are being conducted at sites. Unit leadership regularly review the Emergency preparedness and contingency planning at their sites. We have a standalone standard on emergency preparedness and contingency planning and uniformly applies to all the sites of the Company Hindustan Zinc recognises that Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery is not only an IT subject, rather a business subject. Aligned with this thought, Hindustan Zinc has implemented ISO 22301 Disaster Recovery & Business Continuity Management Framework to prevent the interruption in operations of Hindustan Zinc’s critical IT systems and to ensure that IT systems are continuously available to all the authorised users, all statutory & legal requirements are complied with, and organisation’s finance and reputational interests are protected. Business Continuity Plan (BCP) has considered various risks including technical risk, natural disasters risk, human risk, and risk related to external partners. • Business Continuity Testing & Disaster Recovery Drills are carried out on a half-yearly basis to test the readiness of recovery sites.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DiscloseAnySignificantAdverseI: As part of its due diligence framework, Hindustan Zinc assesses the potential for significant adverse environmental impacts arising from its Tier-1 business partners. The assessment for the year did not identify any high-impact cases. Our supply chain management framework promotes responsible sourcing and enhanced sustainability performance, driven by technology, innovation, and collaboration. Embedded policies support a future ready ecosystem aligned with ESG goals and regulatory compliance. Refer to Our Approach on Supply Chain Management

Leadership Indicators:
Question 1) Note - # This has been reinstated from previous year
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Water Stewardship - Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) at Udaipur & Bhilwara
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Company commissioned a 20 MLD STP in Udaipur in 2014 under a public-private partnership, which is the first of its kind in Rajasthan. Further, Hindustan Zinc Limited and Udaipur Smart City Limited signed an agreement in June 2017 to extend the sewage treatment capacity by another 40 MLD. 25 MLD has been commissioned in January 2019. Another 15 MLD was commissioned in FY2021, taking the total to 60 MLD
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: The replacement of fresh water for operations by STP treated water has led to increased availability of fresh water for the community. 32% of total water withdrawal was satisfied with treated sewage.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Circularity & Waste Management – Filtered Tailing Plant
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: India’s first filtered tailing plant was set up at the Zawar Mines in Rajasthan and in 2024 at Rajpura Dariba Mine. The filtered tailing technology is based on separating water from tailings slurry, which is generated in the beneficiation process. Company repurposes tailings materials and waste rock as backfill to stabilise our underground mining operations, while the remaining tailings are then placed in a specially designed tailings storage to minimise the environmental, social and economic risks. We externally review the integrity/stability of our dam structures and their associated management practices by global experts. The same is in progress in Rampura Agucha Mine.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Key benefits of the filtered tailing technology include recirculation of more than 80% of the process water present in tailings, a faster rehabilitation, and restoration of storage site at mine closure and ensuring re-availability of water for further use.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Water Stewardship - 4 MLD Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) at Zawar and water treatment plant at Rampura Agucha Mines.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Treatment of poor-quality excess water at Zawar Mines by installation of 4000 cum/day effluent treatment plant with zero liquid discharge and recycling in the process. The same has been commissioned at Rampura Agucha Mines.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Reduction of freshwater intake from Tidi Dam on daily basis of c. 3800 cum/day. 2. Being environmentally compliant
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Company executed groundwater recharge intervention project across Hurda, Shahpura, Kotri, and Jahazpur blocks of Bhilwara district. We were able to complete the desilting of ponds, repairing, strengthening and increasing the height of damaged embankments, and construction of 358 recharge shafts for effective groundwater recharge in 83 ponds
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Created a total groundwater recharge potential of over 8.7 million cubic meter/annum
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Climate Change & Energy Transition Agreement for 180 Liquified Natural Gas (LNG) vehicles
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: In a significant move towards green transportation, the Company has joined forces with Greenline, a subsidiary of Essar Group, by signing a contract for 180 LNG vehicles. During the previous year, all 180 LNG vehicles (alternate fuel vehicles) were launched to aid finished goods and interunit transport.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: This initiative is to help reduce Company’s Scope 3 emissions and help achieve SBTi Scope 3 targets.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Instead of conventional hydraulic filling, Company chose to use the paste fill technology. This technology ensured fast filling and practically no bleeding water in the stopes. The other benefits of paste fill technology have been water conservation, better stope stability, surface integrity in mines, and more recycling of tailings.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: - Metal recovery from secondary stopes Reduced cement consumption with increased usage of fly ash • Reduced water consumption as paste solids content is high (77-79% solids) • Utilisation of nearly 39% of tailings in paste fill plant, helping in improving the stability of tailing dam
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: It treats residues produced during smelting process, leading to in-house production of a few key consumables and byproducts, in collaboration with the Company’s central R&D team. Few projects were successfully executed, including production of copper sulphate and zinc sulphate from purification cake as well as production of potassium antimony tartrate from antimony dust.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: It treats different kinds of residues, namely copper dross, purification waste cake, antimony dust/slag, and raw zinc oxide This has also enabled in improvement of metal recoveries of lead, copper and silver.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Circularity & Waste Management - Jarofix Yard Restoration
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Company follows the principle of reducing the waste, quantitatively as well as qualitatively, and performing recovery and recycling. The last priority is disposal in landfills. Company used Mycorrhiza technology for rejuvenation and reclamation of wasteland into productive land by increasing the green cover, enhancing biodiversity, controlling fugitive dust emission, and restoring site. It also makes plants less vulnerable to environmental stresses through optimum use of water resources
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Provides overall stability to waste dump and dump failure due to heavy rains, ensuring safe and stable dump. Phase 1 covered development of green belt in an area of 6.25 hectares (10,000 plants). Further, Phase 2 of area restoration of 16 hectares has been completed, taking the total area to 22.25ha.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Climate Change & Energy Transition Solar Plant
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Company has installed 40.70 MW of solar power project by utilising its waste lands without disturbing any productive land.

In the existing zinc hydro metallurgical process, Jarosite is generated which is treated with lime & cement and disposed in Jarofix Yard. In the Fumer plant, a clean slag will be produced and utilised in cement manufacturing and goethite cake produced will be treated with lime & cement and disposed in captive secured landfill
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Production of usable clean slag and elimination of Jarosite waste of 100758 MT in FY25-26 • Elimination of recurring land for storage of Jarofix • Power generation from waste heat recovery • Increased recovery of zinc, lead, silver, copper, and sulphur • Saving of cement and lime
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Circularity & Waste Management - Jarofix/Jarosite Utilisation in Cement and Road Construction
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Jarosite is waste produced in the hydrometallurgy process of zinc extraction, necessitating additional investment for its stabilisation and disposal. The study showed positive results and encouraged us to conduct field trials. Company has been granted permission by Rajasthan State Pollution Control Board for gainful utilisation of Jarofix waste for road construction/highway construction (embankment). Company also partnered with a few government agencies like NCCBM, NEERI and CRRI for the utilisation of jarosite in cement and road construction.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Jarosite used in cement construction – 1,78,212 MT • Jarofix used in road construction – 4,75,719 MT
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Circularity & Waste Management - Closure of Secured Landfill (SLF) at Vizag
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Company’s Vizag closure and capping was first project in the entire country in which a slurry pond was stabilised, closed and capped as per the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) Guidelines due to introduction of superior geotechnical concepts and material science knowledge.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Nearly 56,000 sqm (6 hectares) of plan area at site has been stabilised and capped as per CPCB guidelines • Aligned to our site closure management standard
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Climate Change & Energy Transition 530 MW Renewable Power
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Hindustan Zinc has extended the power delivery agreement for upto 530 MW renewable energy and it will contribute towards transitioning to green energy and reduce carbon emissions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: It will contribute towards transitioning to green energy and reduce 3.5 mn tCO2e carbon emissions by 2028
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Climate Change & Energy Transition EV bulkers and trucks for Interunit Transport
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Hindustan Zinc has taken a significant leap towards sustainable logistics by signing a contracts with multiple vendors, marking the deployment of 52 electric vehicle (EV) bulker and trucks, each boasting a capacity of 55 metric tonnes and dedicated to the inter-operations transport of concentrates. We have further introduced 5 EV charging stations overall.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: These eco-friendly trucks reaffirm Hindustan Zinc’s commitment to green and sustainable practices. The partnership signifies an important shift in the logistics landscape, aligning with global efforts to reduce carbon emissions and promote cleaner modes of transportation. This marks a total of 52 EVs in Hindustan Zinc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Circularity & Waste Management Zinc Tailings Reprocessing Plant, Rampura Agucha
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: In a landmark step towards circular mining, the Board of Hindustan Zinc approved the establishment of India’s First Zinc tailings reprocessing plant at Rampura Agucha Mine, Bhilwara district, Rajasthan. The plant, with a feed capacity of 10 Mtpa and an investment of up to ₹3823 crore, will extract zinc and silver from previously processed tailings. Contracts have been awarded to CIMIC group companies - Sedgman for engineering and procurement, and Leighton Asia for onshore construction - and the facility is targeted for completion within 28 months from zero date.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Transform legacy mine waste into recoverable zinc and silver, reducing long-term environmental liability • India’s first facility of this kind - sets a national benchmark for responsible tailings management • Directly supports the company’s zero waste to landfill goal by 2030.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Climate Change & Energy Transition EcoZen Low Carbon Zinc - Industrial Partnerships
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Hindustan Zinc scaled the adoption of EcoZen - Asia’s first low-carbon zinc -through strategic partnerships with major industrial customers. HZL partnered with customers to decarbonize the value chain. EcoZen is produced using 100% renewable electricity and carries a carbon footprint of less than 1 tCO2e per tonne of zinc - nearly 75% lower than the global industry average.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Enables customers to significantly reduce their Scope 3 emissions through verified low-carbon zinc sourcing • Strengthens commercial demand for certified green metals in key industrial sectors Positions Hindustan Zinc as the preferred low-carbon metals supplier for decarbonising value chains
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Biodiversity & Ecosystems Baghdarra Crocodile Conservation Reserve
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Hindustan Zinc signed a ₹5 crore MoU with the Forest Department, Udaipur, under the Hariyalo Rajasthan and “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” initiatives for a large scale plantation drive at the Baghdarra Crocodile Conservation Reserve. The Company planted 500 native saplings and committed to nurturing 5,000 more to bolster habitat biodiversity and green cover across the nearly 400-hectare reserve. This public-private collaboration focuses on afforestation, water-conservation nfrastructure, and the development of eco-sensitive visitor zones to protect marsh crocodiles and restore wetland ecosystems.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: 5,000 saplings committed for habitat restoration across the nearly 400-hectare conservation reserve • Strengthens wetland ecosystem resilience and marsh crocodile habitat protection, positively impacting over 200 species in the ecosystem Reinforces the Company’s biodiversity stewardship and No Net Loss commitment under its Biodiversity Management Plan
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Circularity & Waste Management Zinc-Ion Battery Technology - R&D Collaboration
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: As part of its continued collaboration with the Jawaharlal Nehru Centre for Advanced Scientific Research (JNCASR) and Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Madras, Hindustan Zinc is advancing the development of next-generation zinc-ion battery storage solutions. JNCASR has developed and demonstrated stable zinc-ion battery pouch cell prototypes for large-scale renewable energy storage. In parallel, IIT Madras is working on zinc-air battery prototypes at lab scale, with ongoing work focused on vehicle-level range-extension applications. Low-cost electrolyte formulations that address key challenges in cycle life and energy density have been developed through these collaborations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Zinc-ion battery prototypes developed for large-scale renewable energy storage applications • Advances India’s indigenous research in safer, non-flammable energy storage alternatives to lithium-ion Positions zinc as a critical energy transition metal, opening new market opportunities for the Company
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheArea: Chanderiya Lead Zinc Smelter, Dariba Smelting Complex, Rajpura Dariba Mine, Sindesar Khurd Mine, Rampura Agucha Mine, Kayad Mine, Zawar Mines, Zinc Smelter Debari.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple7ExplanatoryText: During the year, there were no adverse orders from regulatory authorities relating to anti-competitive conduct.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce & Industry
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PublicPolicyAdvocated: Area relaxation for base metals (zinc, lead, silver, iron, etc.) in the state of Rajasthan
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MethodResortedForSuchAdvocacy: Advocacy with the State Mines department as well as the Industry Association for the benefit of pioneer companies to get more area for enhancement of production and reduce imports. Relaxation of maximum area limit in exercise of powers conferred under provision 6(1)(b) of MMDR Act. GOI Ministry of Mines order number 16/33/2016-M PART IV issued in September ’25

Representations through National level industry associations/geological conferences and meetings have been sent to apprise the concerned authorities of the process and complexities involved in the exploration of deep-seated base metals.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PublicPolicyAdvocated: Reinstating of QCOs for Lead and Zinc
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MethodResortedForSuchAdvocacy: Refined Zinc (Quality Control) Order, 2025 (S.O. 1768(E), April 17, 2025) and Primary Lead (Quality Control) Order, 2025 (S.O. 1767(E), April 17, 2025) were removed by Ministry of Mines vide order dated November 13th

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: Aligning with Technical Standards (TS-04), structured grievance redressal mechanisms are adhered to for receiving and resolving grievances. In line with sustainability strategy of establishing strong relationships with stakeholders and adhering to the International Finance Corporation (IFC), International Council on Mining and Metals (ICMM), Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD) and United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) Standards for responsible stewardship, United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) Principles & Technical standards, Company has set up a robust Grievance Redressal Mechanism. Regular monitoring and reviewing of the mechanism established is undertaken at various platforms. To further strengthen it, effective stakeholder engagement is a continuous process taken up to reduce the grievances of the stakeholders. Proactive and sustained engagement platforms like “Community Connect” and “Community Leadership Connect” are established to ensure two-way communication network and strengthen the trust element thereby reducing the grievances. A grievance box has been placed outside the plant main gate for stakeholders to share their grievances in written form in the local language. All grievances are required to be resolved in maximum 15 days, failing which they are escalated to the senior management for its resolution and closure. Also, at each Company location, we have well established Social Performance Steering Committee, consisting representatives of major functions and chaired by respective IBU CEO/ SBU Director, through which all grievances are raised and discussed for their timely resolution and thereby reduction in the collective risk involved. Our approach to strengthening Social Performance Steering Committees (SPSC) at business locations involves active participation from functional leaders. This is crucial for monitoring grievances, resolving issues, mitigating key risks, and fostering strong stakeholder relationships. By addressing community feedback and grievances promptly, we reduce collective risks. At the corporate level, the Social Performance Management Committee consolidates and reviews progress at each site, enhancing our overall social performance management.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: There are following mechanisms in place to receive and respond to consumer complaints and feedback: 1. Customer Service Hotline: Vedanta Metal Bazaar is a one-stop portal for customers from buying the product to raising concerns or complaints etc. Customers can raise their concerns or feedback through the portal. Through an automatic raised ticket by the portal, the customers can monitor the progress of their complaints and resolutions thereon. This portal can be accessed through the following link: https://vedantametalbazaar.moglix.com/#/login 2. Email Correspondence: Customers can also reach out to the company via email to lodge complaints or provide feedback. Hindustan Zinc Limited ensures prompt responses to emails, acknowledging receipt and providing a timeline for resolution, whenever possible. By employing these diverse mechanisms, Hindustan Zinc Limited demonstrates its commitment to listening to consumer concerns, addressing issues promptly, and continuously improving its products and services to ensure sustainability and long-term success
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarkForConsumerComplaints: The given number is of formal complaints logged by customers in the Company’s portal related to quality.
The given number is of formal complaints logged by customers in the Company’s portal related to quality.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: Not Applicable, as there are no complaints with respect to advertising, and delivery of essential services; cyber security and data privacy of customers; re-occurrence of instances of product recalls. Further, no penalties were paid or regulatory actions were taken by regulatory authorities on account of the safety of our products/services.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu:
Hindustan Zinc Limited provide products and services related information on its Company’s website and various other platforms as mentioned below. These sites/platforms carry all the factsheets about each of the products we manufacture.
https://www.hzlindia.com/what-we-do/products-powering-transformation https://www.hzlindia.com/uploads/HZL_Product_Brochure_2025_537b31386b.pdf https://www.lme.com/Physical-services/Brands/Approved-brands https://www.lbma.org.uk/good-delivery/silver-current-list#- https://www.hzlindia.com/uploads/Eco_Zen_Product_Leaflet_db978350e6.pdf https://api.environdec.com/api/v1/EPDLibrary/Files/2e5fdc61-b98c-42b9-90d0-08db0d9b78e5/Data
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: Material Safety Data Sheets are issued to all new vendors and are shared with customers on request, ensuring access to complete information on product characteristics, safe use, and handling.Our sales team also interacts with customers and all their queries are resolved during the personal interactions. A dedicated customer services team is available to assist customers with any sales-related services.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu: Hindustan Zinc Limited through the issue of market circulars and in-person interaction with the customers via emails and phone calls inform the customers immediately of any risk of disruption/discontinuation of essential services.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProductInformationOnT: Majority of our production grades are listed on the London Metal Exchange (LME) and therefore following their guidelines for the specification of products on labels, prominent large-size labels are displayed on our product, however, there is no Indian regulatory mandate to display any product information. Hindustan Zinc Limited carried out a consumer/customer/buyer satisfaction survey in FY2024, and one ongoing, with the help of Feedback Insights Agency relating to the major products manufactured by Hindustan Zinc Limited for its significant locations of operation. Feedback Insights team has conducted the survey with the customers of all the products of Zinc, Lead and Silver in domestic & export regions. They have done this survey by various means such as in-person interactions, telephonic & web-based discussions to understand their satisfaction, and loyalty levels by assessing the parameters like product, packaging, delivery, contracting process, complaint handling, etc., to derive the performance score.
